diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Somatic/anxiety-related RUQ discomfort on a background of mild autoimmune hepatitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild vague ache fits a somatic/functional origin.
RUQ location overlaps but severity/character atypical for cholecystitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain reproduced by palpation and temporally locked to the alarming SMS

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Somatic/anxiety-related RUQ discomfort on a background of mild autoimmune hepatitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pressure-reproducible pain arising immediately after an anxiety trigger strongly suggests a somatic/musculoskeletal-functional origin.
Absence of fever/rigors makes biliary sepsis unlikely.
No jaundice/encephalopathy/coagulopathy argues strongly against acute liver failure.
No paracetamol overdose or hepatotoxin lowers the risk of an acute toxic hepatitis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Repeat/complete LFTs with bilirubin and INR
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantify the abnormality, assess synthetic function (INR) and bilirubin — arranged via GP within days.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Autoimmune liver serology (ANA, SMA, anti-LKM), immunoglobulins
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Work-up of suspected autoimmune hepatitis in the outpatient setting.
Assess for biliary pathology electively; urgent only if biliary-sepsis features develop.
